successCriteria.description: src/modules/health/health.model.ts exists and exports a HealthState type alias defined as 'HEALTHY' | 'DEGRADED' | 'UNHEALTHY'
src/modules/health/health.model.ts exports a HealthStatus interface with exactly three fields: status (HealthState), checkedAt (Date), message (string | undefined)
src/modules/health/health.service.interface.ts exists and exports an IHealthService interface with a single method getHealth(): HealthStatus
health.service.interface.ts imports HealthStatus from './health.model' following the same import pattern as uptime.service.interface.ts imports from './uptime.model'
Both new files compile without errors under the project's strict TypeScript configuration (strict: true, noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks)

constraints: Follow the exact structural pattern from src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts and src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts (interface exports, import style)
TypeScript strict mode compliance: strict: true, noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks must all pass
No 'any' types — use 'unknown' with type guards if needed (project constraint: no-any)
Files must be created in src/modules/health/ directory following the project's modular structure convention
outOfScope: Phase 3 — Health routes and module index (src/modules/health/index.ts, health.routes.ts)
Phase 4 — Version domain model and service interface (src/modules/version/)
Phase 6 — Version routes and module index (src/modules/version/index.ts, version.routes.ts)
Phase 7 — Register health and version routes in app (src/app.ts updates)
Health service implementation logic (actual health check logic, dependency checks, etc.)
Route registration and HTTP endpoint exposure
